name: Wire quiz-adaptive.ts into the QuizArcade batch endpoint
prismaModelSnippet:
// Add to packages/data/prisma/schema.prisma, near ReputationProfile (same
// tenant/chat/user shape — one row per (chat, user)).
model QuizDifficultyState {
  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId       String
  chatId         String
  telegramUserId BigInt
  level          Int      @default(1)
  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime @updatedAt

  @@unique([chatId, telegramUserId])
  @@index([tenantId, chatId])
  @@map("quiz_difficulty_states")
}
botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes: Nothing goes into apps/bot/src/bot-update.service.ts for this task — the adaptive quiz lives entirely in the Mini App's HTTP API (apps/api/src/games/*), not in the bot's command dispatcher. The botUpdateServiceSnippet field above is repurposed (per the task's own instruction) to carry the two real target files instead: apps/api/src/games/games.service.ts (quizBatch method + two import lines + one field) and apps/api/src/games/games.controller.ts (the "quiz" POST handler). Apply both together — the controller's new call signature only compiles once the service's quizBatch signature is extended. The Prisma model (QuizDifficultyState) must be added to packages/data/prisma/schema.prisma and `prisma generate` run before packages/data/src/quiz-difficulty-repository.ts's Prisma-backed class will typecheck against the real client — right now `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -p packages/data/tsconfig.json --noEmit` reports `Property 'quizDifficultyState' does not exist on type 'PrismaClient'` for it, the exact same pre-existing shape of error already present for other not-yet-generated pending models on this branch (adminDecision, ecaRule, knownAdmin) — so this is expected, not a regression. The new repository file's own test (quiz-difficulty-repository.test.ts, InMemory-only) passes standalone via vitest independent of that Prisma-generate step. No existing test file needed edits for the repository; games.service.test.ts's existing quizBatch tests keep passing unchanged since all new params are appended with defaults, but a new assertion on `batch.difficulty` should be added once the controller/service snippet is applied.
risks: Low risk, additive-only: (1) the new Prisma model/repository is a brand-new disjoint table, no migration touches existing data; (2) quizBatch's new params are all optional/defaulted, so the two untouched existing call sites (games.service.test.ts) keep compiling and passing as-is; (3) the quiz endpoint's behavior for anyone who never sends `answers` is byte-for-byte identical to today except for one added `difficulty` field in the response, which existing web clients simply ignore. The one real gap is functional, not risk: difficulty is tracked and returned but cannot yet influence which questions are served, because the 5000+-question trivia bank has no difficulty tags set (the field exists on the type but is unused everywhere in trivia-bank.ts) — until that bank is tagged (or a numeric difficulty added), "adaptive" quiz only adapts a number shown to the client, not the actual question mix. Also flagged but intentionally left undone: apps/web's quizBatch()/QuizArcade.tsx don't yet send `answers` or surface `difficulty`, since wiring shared web files was outside this task's stated 3-part scope.
